slides.title: Make the pitch easy to follow
slides.subtitle: A short deck should help judges understand what was built and why it matters.

visual_aid.purpose: Open with a simple framing statement.

speaker_notes.key_message: Clarity comes before detail in a short pitch.
talk_track: Start by telling the team that judges need the product, user, and value quickly.
This is a structure for clarity, not a guarantee of winning.
avoid_saying: This outline works for every hackathon.

slides.title: Start with the user problem
body: Explain the problem in plain language.
Keep the opening short enough to leave time for the demo.
Avoid starting with the technology stack.

visual_aid.purpose: Use short bullets to keep the opening clear.

speaker_notes.key_message: The audience needs to understand the user before the implementation.
talk_track: Ask the team to state who the user is.
Then state what changes for that user.
Leave implementation detail for the demo and questions.

slides.title: Show what you built
body: Describe the product in one sentence.
Show the main input and output.
Explain what judges should watch during the demo.

speaker_notes.key_message: A short pitch should make the product understandable before it tries to prove everything.
talk_track: Point judges to the main result they should evaluate.
Move extra feature detail to backup.

visual_aid.purpose: Summarize judging criteria in a compact way.

validation.expected: Three criteria rows fit on one slide.
validation.fallback: Use the actual event rubric if available.

speaker_notes.key_message: Use judging criteria to decide what proof to show.
talk_track: Explain that organizers define judging criteria and flow.
Tell teams to replace this table with their event rubric.
Do not imply these are the only possible criteria.

slides.title: Prepare for questions
body: Keep a screenshot or recording ready.
Know which technical choice was hardest.
Bring extra details only if judges ask.

speaker_notes.key_message: Backup proof helps the conversation continue if the live path breaks.
talk_track: Remind teams to keep proof ready.
Name the hard technical part plainly.
Do not invent numbers if they were not measured.
avoid_saying: Our project is the best without evidence.

slides.title: End with a clear next step
slides.quote: We built this for this user, here is the proof, and here is what we need next.

speaker_notes.key_message: The close should help judges remember the project and ask a useful question.
talk_track: Use the sentence as the final check.
If a slot cannot be filled honestly, improve the evidence before presenting.
